channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: What to know about US military strikes on alleged drug boats
item.description: The U.S. military strikes on alleged drug boats have killed over 200 people. The attacks began in September and aim to stem drug flow, but critics question their legality and effectiveness.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military strikes on alleged drug boats have killed over 200 people. The attacks began in September and aim to stem drug flow, but critics question their legality and effectiveness.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Russian attack on Ukraine kills at least 11 and traps others in damaged buildings
item.description: Russia attacked Ukraine overnight Tuesday, killing at least 11 people and trapping others, authorities said. Residential buildings and other civilian infrastructure were damaged in eight of Kyiv's districts.
item.content:encoded:
Russia attacked Ukraine overnight Tuesday, killing at least 11 people and trapping others, authorities said. Residential buildings and other civilian infrastructure were damaged in eight of Kyiv's districts.

(Image credit: Efrem Lukatsky)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Iran halts talks with U.S. over Israeli actions in Lebanon, Gaza
item.description: Trumps says Israel and Hezbollah will stop fighting after Iran stops talks with U.S. over Israeli operations in southern Lebanon, Gaza
item.content:encoded:
Trumps says Israel and Hezbollah will stop fighting after Iran stops talks with U.S. over Israeli operations in southern Lebanon, Gaza

(Image credit: STR/AP Photo/undefined)
item.dc:creator: Hadeel Al-Shalchi

item.title: U.S. strikes Iranian military sites. And, Trump's $1.8 billion fund faces scrutiny
item.description: American aircraft fired on a number of Iranian sites over the weekend, including Qeeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz. And, Trump's anti-weaponization fund faces scrutiny in Congress and the courts.
item.content:encoded:
American aircraft fired on a number of Iranian sites over the weekend, including Qeeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz. And, Trump's anti-weaponization fund faces scrutiny in Congress and the courts.

(Image credit: Kawnat Haju)
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Nuyen

item.title: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo near 300 as survivors describe their recoveries
item.description: The outbreak remains focused in Congo's eastern Ituri province. Congo has reported over 1,000 suspected cases with the Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatment or vaccine.
item.content:encoded:
The outbreak remains focused in Congo's eastern Ituri province. Congo has reported over 1,000 suspected cases with the Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatment or vaccine.

(Image credit: Moses Sawasawa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Funds for Trump's Board of Peace aren't in World Bank account. So where are they?
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Financial Times reporter Abigail Hauslohner about the funding of President Trump's Board of Peace to oversee Gaza's reconstruction.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Financial Times reporter Abigail Hauslohner about the funding of President Trump's Board of Peace to oversee Gaza's reconstruction.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: After Russian missile strike, owners of Ukrainian coffee shop pledge to rebuild
item.description: After a large-scale Russian missile strike hit downtown Kyiv, the owners of a coffee shop that opened just hours earlier were already serving coffee again and planning to rebuild.
item.content:encoded: After a large-scale Russian missile strike hit downtown Kyiv, the owners of a coffee shop that opened just hours earlier were already serving coffee again and planning to rebuild.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Palamarenko

item.title: Violent clashes give way to peaceful PSG parade after Champions League win
item.description: A huge crowd of supporters gathered peacefully near the Eiffel Tower on Sunday to celebrate Paris Saint-Germain's victory, which was marred by violent clashes overnight that led police to detain hundreds of people.
item.content:encoded:
A huge crowd of supporters gathered peacefully near the Eiffel Tower on Sunday to celebrate Paris Saint-Germain's victory, which was marred by violent clashes overnight that led police to detain hundreds of people.

(Image credit: Emma Da Silva)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Right-wing candidate pulls ahead in first round of Colombia's presidential vote
item.description: Tough-on-crime outsider Aberaldo de la Espriella took the lead in Colombia's presidential race on Sunday night, setting up a runoff with Iván Cepeda, an ally of outgoing President Gustavo Petro.
item.content:encoded:
Tough-on-crime outsider Aberaldo de la Espriella took the lead in Colombia's presidential race on Sunday night, setting up a runoff with Iván Cepeda, an ally of outgoing President Gustavo Petro.

(Image credit: Fernando Vergara)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. bombs Iranian military sites, then downs missiles Tehran fired at troops in Kuwait
item.description: The U.S. says it has bombed radar and drone sites in Iran after Tehran shot down an American drone over the weekend. Iran then said it targeted American soldiers in Kuwait with missiles, which the U.S. says it shot down on Monday.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. says it has bombed radar and drone sites in Iran after Tehran shot down an American drone over the weekend. Iran then said it targeted American soldiers in Kuwait with missiles, which the U.S. says it shot down on Monday.

(Image credit: Amirhosein Khorgooi/ISNA)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
